# KDBX test fixtures

Real KeePassXC-authored vaults used by the FIX-style tests (decode bytes
captured from a real file → expected values). Password for all: `fixturepass`.

Regenerate (KeePassXC 2.7.x CLI at
`/Applications/KeePassXC.app/Contents/MacOS/keepassxc-cli`):

```bash
CLI=/Applications/KeePassXC.app/Contents/MacOS/keepassxc-cli

# kpxc_kdbx3_aeskdf.kdbx — KeePassXC-native AES-KDF vault.
# NOTE: db-create with AES-KDF produces a *KDBX 3.1* file (AES-KDF predates
# KDBX4). Used as a boundary fixture: our KDBX4 reader must reject major v3.
printf 'fixturepass\nfixturepass\n' | "$CLI" db-create -p -t 100 kpxc_kdbx3_aeskdf.kdbx
printf 'fixturepass\nentrypw1\n'   | "$CLI" add -u user@example.com -p kpxc_kdbx3_aeskdf.kdbx Sample

# kpxc_argon2.kdbx — Argon2d vault, authored by our spike then re-saved by
# KeePassXC (so the bytes are KeePassXC's serializer output, Argon2d preserved)
( cd ../../../spike/kdbx_spike && dart run bin/kdbx4.dart write /tmp/fx.kdbx fixturepass )
cp /tmp/fx.kdbx kpxc_argon2.kdbx
printf 'fixturepass\nentrypw2\n' | "$CLI" add -u user2@example.com -p kpxc_argon2.kdbx Current/Sample2
```

The files contain random seeds, so regenerated bytes differ each run; the tests
assert *structure* (signatures, version, cipher/KDF UUIDs, param presence and
types) and round-trip fidelity, not exact bytes.
